frameworks.name: global view of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: global-workspace-style account argued to be insufficient on its own

frameworks.name: local view of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: local-recurrence account argued to be insufficient on its own

frameworks.name: synthesis (centrist) view
frameworks.one_line: integrative position combining global and local accounts

frameworks.name: artificial intelligence research
frameworks.one_line: related AI ideas cited as context for machine consciousness

core_question: What theory of consciousness is consistent with reviewed evidence on NCC, attention, function, and non-human consciousness?
key_assumptions: prior chapters' reviews of NCC, attention, function, and animal/robot consciousness constrain any adequate theory
consciousness theory must address both neural and functional levels
interpretations: neither purely global nor purely local theories of consciousness suffice
the evidence motivates a centrist synthesis spanning the two views
out_of_scope_note: Book-chapter synthesis/manifesto (Oxford volume); no empirical findings or manipulations — reflective/integrative summary of prior chapters.
